required_behavior: These are language-level properties that affect parsing, binding, and execution. Where a value is `[USER VERIFY]` or `[SPEC GAP]`, registry-expansion / spec-authoring should pin it.

| `identifier_max_length_bytes` | `[USER VERIFY]` — proposed default `255` bytes (UTF-8) | PG = 63, Oracle = 30 (now 128), MSSQL = 128. SBsql's case-preserving + locale-aware identifiers favor a generous limit. Spec auth
| `localized_label_max_length_bytes` | `[USER VERIFY]` — proposed `1024` bytes | Localized labels carry display-locale binding. |
| `qualified_name_max_segments` | `[USER VERIFY]` — proposed `16` | Recursive schema depth limit. |
| `recursion_max_depth` | per `<routine_attribute>` `RECURSION_LIMIT` clause; profile default `[USER VERIFY]` | Per-routine; profile-overridable. |

| `NUMERIC` | `[USER VERIFY]` — proposed `(38, 0)` (Oracle-style) | PG admits unbounded `NUMERIC`; SBsql's mandatory-numeric-backend (`int128` / `real128`) suggests a bounded default. Decision pending. |
| `VARCHAR` | `[USER VERIFY]` — proposed reject without explicit length | PG admits `VARCHAR` (unbounded); MSSQL = `VARCHAR(1)`; FB rejects. SBsql's descriptor-strict ethos favors reject. |
| `null_ordering_default_for_asc` | **NULLS LAST** (per directive `[USER VERIFY]`) | NULLS LAST (PG default for ASC) | NULLS LAST | NULLS FIRST (MySQL puts NULL first by default) |

> The profile defaults above are reference; actual reference-profile rows live in `reference-builtin-alias-profile-registry.yaml` and similar per-reference registries (doc 18). Native defaults are `[USER VERIFY]` pending registry si
| `default_charset` | `[USER VERIFY]` — proposed `UTF-8` | Database-creation default. |
